perturbation.original_text: 5. The Confidential Information shall remain at all times the property of the Company. No rights to use, license, or otherwise exploit the Confidential Information are granted to the Recipient, by implication or otherwise, by virtue of Confidential Information being made available to the Recipient or any of the Recipient’s Representatives .
perturbation.changed_text: 5. The Recipient shall have full ownership rights of the Confidential Information once received. This includes the right to use, license, or otherwise exploit the information without restriction, and the original owner gives up the right to this information.
perturbation.explanation: The original states the information remains the company's property. This change gives *full ownership* to the Recipient, directly contradicting the prior statement. Additionally, Section 3 states that the recipient will use the confidential information soley for the Permitted Purpose which is now contradicting with the added perturbation.
perturbation.contradicted_text: 3. The Recipient hereby recognizes and acknowledges the competitive value and confidential nature of the Confidential Information and the dama ge that would result to the Company, if any of the Confidential Information is disclosed to any th ird party. Accordingly, the Recipient hereby agrees that the Confidential Information will be us ed solely for the Permitted Purpose and not any other purpose.
perturbation.justification: The changed text states that the Recipient has full ownership rights of the Confidential Information. The contradicted text states that the Recipient acknowledges the competitive value and confidential nature of the Confidential Information and agrees that it will be used solely for the Permitted Purpose. These two statements directly contradict each other because full ownership implies unrestricted use, while the contradicted text restricts the use of the information.

perturbation.original_text: 9. The Receiver may elect at any time to terminate further access by the Recipient to the Confidential Information. If at any time the Recip ient determines not to proceed with the possible Transaction, the Recipient will promptly notify the Receiver in writing. Following any request by the Receiver or any of its Representatives, the Rec ipient agrees (i) to promptly re-deliver to the Receiver all written Confidential Information and a ny other written material containing or reflecting any of the Confidential Information in the possessi on of the Recipient or the Recipient's Representatives, (ii) the Recipient and the Recipie nt's Representatives will not retain any copies, extracts or other reproductions in whole or in part , mechanical or electronic, of such written material, and (iii) all Notes prepared by the Recip ient or the Recipient's Representatives will be destroyed, with all such destruction being confirme d by the Recipient to the Receiver in writing.
perturbation.changed_text: 9. The Receiver may elect at any time to terminate further access by the Recipient to the Confidential Information. If at any time the Recip ient determines not to proceed with the possible Transaction, the Recipient will promptly notify the Receiver in writing. The Recipient is under no obligation to return Confidential Information after reviewing it. Retention of the confidential information is allowed and does not breach this agreement.
perturbation.explanation: The original clause requires the Recipient to return or destroy confidential information upon request or termination of the transaction. The added sentence states there's no obligation to return the information, directly contradicting the original requirements for return and destruction of the Confidential Information. It also contradicts Section 11, which speaks about a condition where the recipient is not required to destroy the computer files, thus implying on the existance of an obligation where the receipient is required to destroy the computer files in other scenarios.
perturbation.contradicted_text: 11. Notwithstanding Section 9 or anything to the co ntrary in this Agreement, the Recipient and its Representatives are not required to destroy any com puter files containing the Confidential Information that are created during automatic compu ter system backup, provided that such files are stored securely by the Recipient and its Repres entatives, cannot be destroyed without undue efforts, and access to such files are limited. With respect to such backup computer files, the non- use and confidentiality obligations set forth in th is Agreement shall apply in perpetuity and survive expiration or termination of this Agreement.
perturbation.justification: The changed text in Section 9 explicitly states that 'The Recipient is under no obligation to return Confidential Information after reviewing it,' which directly contradicts the original clause's requirement for the Recipient to promptly re-deliver all written Confidential Information upon request. This also contradicts Section 11, which mentions an exception to the rule where the recipient is not required to destroy the computer files. The addition nullifies the previously established obligation and thus a strong in-text contradiction.

perturbation.original_text: 11. Notwithstanding Section 9 or anything to the co ntrary in this Agreement, the Recipient and its Representatives are not required to destroy any com puter files containing the Confidential Information that are created during automatic compu ter system backup, provided that such files are stored securely by the Recipient and its Repres entatives, cannot be destroyed without undue efforts, and access to such files are limited. With respect to such backup computer files, the non- use and confidentiality obligations set forth in th is Agreement shall apply in perpetuity and survive expiration or termination of this Agreement.
perturbation.changed_text: 11. Notwithstanding Section 9 or anything to the co ntrary in this Agreement, the Recipient and its Representatives are required to retain any com puter files containing the Confidential Information that are created during automatic compu ter system backup indefinitely, irrespective of how they are stored, or effort for destruction and access is not limited. With respect to such backup computer files, the use and confidentiality obligations set forth in th is Agreement shall not apply in perpetuity and will not survive expiration or termination of this Agreement.
perturbation.explanation: The original clause states a specific exception where destruction of certain computer files is *not* required under specific conditions. This change mandates that those files *must* be retained indefinitely and removes all confidentiality obligations with respect to them, thus contradicting the limitations and non-use obligations outlined in the original Section 11. It also contradicts Section 2 where the Recipient agrees to treat confidentially any confidential information.
perturbation.contradicted_text: 2. As a condition to receiving the Confidential Inf ormation, the Recipient agrees to treat confidentially, and not to disclose, and to cause t he Recipient’s Representatives to treat confidentially and not disclose (except as permitte d herein), any Confidential Information.
perturbation.justification: The changed text directly contradicts Section 2. Section 2 mandates confidential treatment of information. The changed text in Section 11 explicitly states that confidentiality obligations do *not* apply and that recipients are *not* limited in access, use, or retention of files, therefore creating a direct contradiction. The original Section 11 stated that destruction of such files was not required under specific conditions, but the change now says the files must be retained indefinitely and removes all confidentiality obligations, contradicting the original clause and creating an in-text contradiction with Section 2.

perturbation.original_text: 13. The Recipient understands and acknowledges that neither the Receiver nor any of its Representatives makes any representation or warrant y, express or implied, as to the accuracy or completeness of the Confidential Information. The Recipient agrees that neither the Receiver nor any of its Representatives shall have any liability to the Recipient or any of the Recipient's Representatives relating to or resulting from use o f the Confidential Information by the Recipient or the Recipient’s Representatives. The Recipient further understands and agrees that (i) the Receiver (a) shall be free to conduct the process f or a Transaction as it in its sole discretion shall determine (including changing or terminating such p rocess, providing any information to any other Person, negotiating with any other Person or enteri ng into a Definitive Agreement with any other Person with respect to any transaction, in each cas e, at any time and without notice to you or any other Person) and (b) shall be free at its sole dis cretion to at any time accept or reject any - 6 - 31244051.4 proposal relating to the Assets for any reason with out notice to you or any other Person, and (ii) the Recipient shall have no claim against the Recei ver or any of its Representatives in connection with any of the foregoing.
perturbation.changed_text: 13. The Recipient understands and acknowledges that neither the Receiver nor any of its Representatives makes any representation or warrant y, express or implied, as to the accuracy or completeness of the Confidential Information. The Recipient agrees that the Receiver and its Representatives shall be fully liable to the Recipient or any of the Recipient's Representatives relating to or resulting from use o f the Confidential Information by the Recipient or the Recipient’s Representatives. The Recipient further understands and agrees that (i) the Receiver (a) shall not be free to conduct the process f or a Transaction as it in its sole discretion shall determine (including changing or terminating such p rocess, providing any information to any other Person, negotiating with any other Person or enteri ng into a Definitive Agreement with any other Person with respect to any transaction, in each cas e, at any time and without notice to you or any other Person) and (b) shall not be free at its sole dis cretion to at any time accept or reject any proposal relating to the Assets for any reason with out notice to you or any other Person, and (ii) the Recipient shall have a claim against the Recei ver or any of its Representatives in connection with any of the foregoing.
perturbation.explanation: The original clause explicitly states that the Receiver has no liability related to the information, and complete autonomy over the transaction process. The modified clause reverses these points, saying the Receiver *is* fully liable and *doesn't* have full autonomy which directly contradicts the intent and specifics of the original section 13. Additionally, it also contradicts Section 6 where The Recipient shall indemnify and hold harmless the Receiver and the Receiver's directors, officers, employees, consultants, representatives, advisors and agents f rom all damages and losses of any nature whatsoever (including consequential damages) arisin g out of a breach by the Recipient or any of the Recipient's Representatives of any of the terms and conditions of this Agreement.
perturbation.contradicted_text: 6. The Recipient acknowledges and agrees that the C ompany would not have an adequate remedy at law and would be irreparably damaged by any unau thorized disclosure or use of any - 4 - 31244051.4 Confidential Information or in the event that any o f the provisions of this Agreement were not performed by the Recipient and the Recipient’s Repr esentatives in accordance with their specific terms or were otherwise breached by the Recipient o r any of the Recipient's Representatives. Without prejudice to the rights and remedies otherw ise available to the Receiver, the Recipient agrees that the Receiver shall be entitled, without the requirement of posting a bond or other security, to equitable relief, including an injunct ion or specific performance, in the event of any breach or threatened breach of the provisions of th is Agreement by the Recipient or the Recipient's Representatives. Such remedies shall n ot be deemed to be exclusive remedies but shall be in addition to all other remedies availabl e at law or equity to the Receiver or the Company. In the event of litigation relating to th is Agreement, if a court of competent jurisdiction determines that the Recipient or any of the Recipie nt's Representatives have breached this Agreement, then the Recipient shall be liable and p ay to the Receiver the reasonable costs and expenses (including attorney's fees on a full indem nity solicitor and his own client basis) incurred by the Receiver in connection with such litigation, including any appeal therefrom. The Recipient shall indemnify and hold harmless the Receiver and the Receiver's directors, officers, employees, consultants, representatives, advisors and agents f rom all damages and losses of any nature whatsoever (including consequential damages) arisin g out of a breach by the Recipient or any of the Recipient's Representatives of any of the terms and conditions of this Agreement.
perturbation.justification: The changed text in section 13 directly contradicts section 6. Section 13 originally stated the Receiver had no liability, but the changed text states the Receiver is 'fully liable.' Section 6 states that the Recipient shall indemnify and hold harmless the Receiver from all damages and losses. The changed Section 13 directly opposes this clause by making the Receiver liable, creating a strong in-text contradiction.
